intro: The clearest split is that Agora uses a freemium model and Yjs uses a free model. Agora is Real-time audio, video, and interactive live streaming SDK with global SD-RTN network infrastructure; Yjs is CI/CD for Applications tool for developers. Specializes in Collaborative Editing (CRDT). The data points Agora at chat applications and live collaboration features, and Yjs at automated build/test/deploy pipelines and team collaboration on code. Pricing: Agora is freemium, free tier; Yjs is free, free tier. Lock-in is low for Agora and low for Yjs, with DX at 5/5 vs 5/5 and transparency at 4/5 vs 5/5. On listed strengths, Agora emphasises chat applications and live collaboration features, and Yjs emphasises managed pub/sub infrastructure removes server ops burden. Only Agora lists an enterprise tier in the data. Honest trade-off: Agora is not for static projects; Yjs is not for purely request-response apps with no real-time needs, so confirm the fit first, then weigh the pivotal factor above against your budget and lock-in tolerance.
quick_take: Agora is for chat applications; Yjs is for apps with live features: chat, collaboration, gaming; decide based on which pricing model fits your budget.
verdict_a: Choose Agora if a freemium pricing model fits your procurement and your workload matches chat applications. Skip it if your context is static projects.
verdict_b: Choose Yjs if a free pricing model fits your procurement and your workload matches apps with live features: chat, collaboration, gaming. Skip it if your context is purely request-response apps with no real-time needs.

faq.a: Entry pricing is listed as unknown for Agora and unknown for Yjs. Both offer a free tier for getting started. Total cost at scale depends on usage and plan details that are not in this dataset, so confirm with each vendor.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Agora to Yjs?
faq.a: Agora is tagged with low lock-in (proprietary sd-rtn network and signaling; switching requires rewrite). Yjs is tagged with low lock-in (oss crdt library). Both sides are low lock-in, so a migration is feasible but still needs planning around data export and SDK rewrites.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, Agora or Yjs?
faq.a: Both are rated 5/5 for developer experience in this dataset. With equal scores, the choice comes down to the specific SDK surface and docs you will use day to day. Run a short spike on each before deciding.

faq.q: Is Yjs a good alternative to Agora?
faq.a: Yjs is listed as fitting apps with live features: chat, collaboration, gaming, which overlaps with but is not identical to Agora's scope. Yjs is flagged as not for purely request-response apps with no real-time needs, so confirm your context does not fall there.
